security.title: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
security.description: The product specifies permissions for a security-critical resource in a way that allows that resource to be read or modified by unintended actors.

evidence.explanation: application.properties enables the H2 database console unconditionally via 'spring.h2.console.enabled=true' with no Spring Security configuration present in the codebase. The H2 console is a critical resource (full database read/write access) that is accessible to any unauthenticated user who can reach the server, allowing unintended actors to read or modify all application data.

security.title: Insufficient Logging
security.description: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.

evidence.explanation: Security-critical state-changing operations produce no log entries. IncidentService.report() persists new safety incidents without any logging. ReactorController.create() (POST /api/reactors) creates reactor records with zero logging. Neither the controller nor service layer logs successful or failed mutations to these safety-critical resources. Only the LegacyAuditFilter logs the bare HTTP method and URI, omitting outcomes, actors, and payload details needed for a meaningful audit trail.
